description_long: This object is a detailed model of a crayfish or freshwater lobster, featuring a segmented body with a robust, reddish-brown exoskeleton. The cephalothorax is broad and armored, with two large, textured claws (chelae) extending forward, each covered in fine bumps and color gradations from brown to greenish hues. The abdomen is clearly segmented, tapering to a fan-shaped tail with visible swimmerets underneath. Multiple walking legs extend from the thorax, and two long, slender antennae project from the head, accompanied by smaller antennules. The eyes are set on short stalks, positioned laterally on the head, giving the crustacean a wide field of view. The surface appears hard and slightly rough, with a matte to semi-gloss finish, and the coloration transitions from orange-brown on the body to greenish and bluish tones on the claws.
description: A realistic crayfish with a segmented reddish-brown body, large textured claws, and long antennae. The surface is hard, slightly rough, and features color gradients from brown to greenish-blue.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the crayfish, highlighting the large right claw, segmented abdomen, and extended antennae.
description_view_1: Top-down view displaying the symmetrical arrangement of claws, walking legs, and the segmentation of the abdomen and tail.
description_view_2: Opposite side view emphasizing the left claw, the curvature of the body, and the detailed segmentation of the tail.
description_view_3: Frontal view focusing on the two large claws, the head with visible eyes on stalks, and the forward-projecting antennae.
